contract.goal: Add the typed layer + pure READ path for the consolidated manifest.toml, sitting on U1's parseToml. Read-only — no writer (U3), no verb wiring (U3/U5). The 'reader before writer' setup unit: prove the typed shape reconstructs off a real manifest.toml before anything writes one.
acceptance_criteria: types.ts gains ManifestMeta (schema_version, title, slug, started, status, current_branch, latest_checkin, strategy) and ManifestToml = { meta, config: Config, phases: ManifestPhase[], events: Event[], checkins: Checkin[], sessions: Session[] }; additive only, existing Manifest/Config/etc untouched
new manifest-toml.ts exports readManifest(raw: string): ManifestToml — calls parseToml, validates + reconstructs the typed tree

[meta] and [config] required (missing/wrong-typed required key throws loud LoomError naming the key); [config] omits schema_version (deduped to meta), readManifest synthesizes config.schema_version from meta

[[phases]]/[[events]]/[[checkins]]/[[sessions]] each absent->[]; [[events]] lenient ({at,event,detail}, detail kept as parsed record, no per-variant validation); [[checkins]]/[[sessions]] reconstruct full nested shape from inline tables with minimal structural validation

real-artifact regression: a hand-authored genuine manifest.toml fixture (mirrors this project; a [[checkins]] with a populated contract carrying torture chars # / quote / */ / commas, and [[events]] with empty vs populated detail) parsed via readManifest asserting structural survival
strip-only smoke manifest-toml.smoke.ts imports readManifest directly + runs on the fixture, shelled via spawnSync('node') asserting exit 0 + marker
vitest green (new + full loom suite) + standalone node manifest-toml.smoke.ts exit 0
rules_applied: Structure strict, semantics lenient: validate presence/type loudly (require* family naming the key); trust string-literal-union fields (status/result via cast) and Event.detail (kept as parsed record) — operator's lenient choice
Additive / non-breaking: no edits to the live manifest.ts or any verb; the old manifest.json read path keeps working until U5
Harvest jelly-loom's requireString/requireStringArray validator posture; extend the family (requireNumber, requireNumberArray, requireTable, nullableString, optionalString)
null encoded by absence: TOML has no null literal, so nullable scalars (current_branch, latest_checkin) and optional phase fields read back from key omission
Type the full six-section target now (operator decision); absent sections degrade to [] rather than erroring
disqualifiers: editing the live manifest.ts or verbs (U3/U5)
a glob-floor real-artifact test (no corpus exists yet — fixture-based regression only)
silent mis-read (must throw loud naming the key)
gold-plating validation beyond the typed shape
inputs: this project's live manifest.json + config.json + a real checkin (fixture grounding)

actions: types.ts: added ManifestMeta (the [meta] scalar fields) + ManifestToml (composes the existing per-record types as the six sections); additive (+37/-0), legacy single-file types untouched

manifest-toml.ts: readManifest(raw) calls parseToml then reconstructs the typed tree. require* family (requireTable/String/Number/StringArray/NumberArray + nullableString/optionalString + asTable + sectionTables) validates STRUCTURE loudly (LoomError naming section+key); reconstructMeta/Config/StringMap/Phase/Event/Checkin/Session build the typed sections. [meta]/[config] required; schema_version deduped to meta and synthesized onto Config; events lenient (detail kept as parsed record); absent array sections -> []

fixtures/manifest-real.toml: hand-authored genuine manifest mirroring this project — torture checkin (goal with # / quote / */ / commas), empty-detail (project-initialized) + populated-detail (pr-opened) events, a phase with nested pr inline table + a phase omitting optionals
manifest-toml.smoke.ts: strip-only node smoke importing readManifest, running on the fixture, asserting shape, printing a marker

manifest-toml.test.ts: real-artifact tier (full typed shape + torture checkin byte-exact survival), unit tier (null-via-absence, present nullable, absent sections, optional phase fields, lenient detail), loud-rejection tier (8 expectReject cases asserting LoomError + code + naming fragment), spawnSync('node') smoke
Verified: npx vitest run plugins/loom = 15 files / 208 passed; manifest-toml.test.ts isolated = 16 passed; node plugins/loom/cli/lib/manifest-toml.smoke.ts exit 0 with marker
Evaluator panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming) returned approved, zero blocking/advisory findings

changes_since_previous: Second unit of Phase 2. Builds the typed read path on top of U1's parseToml: ManifestToml type + readManifest, validated against a hand-authored real manifest.toml. Reader-before-writer — U3 adds the atomic write path + migrates the writer verbs onto this layer; U4 folds checkins/sessions in via the same inline-table mechanism.
notes_for_pr: Operator decisions this unit (grill before contract): (1) type ALL SIX sections now (vs deferring checkins/sessions to U4) — define the full target once; (2) LENIENT event-detail narrowing (validate at/event + that detail is a table, keep detail as the parsed record) vs per-variant validation; (3) name the top-level type ManifestToml (vs LoomState / evolving Manifest).

Validation principle: STRUCTURE strict (loud LoomError naming the key on missing/wrong-typed), SEMANTICS lenient (string-literal unions like status/result read via cast; Event.detail trusted). This is the line that keeps the typing layer from gold-plating while still guaranteeing the return type.

null encoding: TOML has no null literal, so meta.current_branch / meta.latest_checkin (and optional phase fields) are encoded by KEY ABSENCE — absent nullable scalar reads null, absent optional reads undefined. U3's writer must omit these keys when null/undefined (flagging here so the write path honors the same convention).

Panel = contract-fit + test-unit + naming. Same manual override as U1: derive-panel adds evaluator-react-api for these pure-Node .ts files (no React/JSX) — dropped. Substrate-followup: gate react-api on JSX/react-import presence.

Real-artifact scope: the fixture IS the regression (no real manifest.toml corpus exists during Phase 2). The glob-floor test over a real corpus is deferred to post-migration (Phase 2 cleanup / Phase 3), per the testing-strategy whiteboard — do NOT write a .skip'd floor-against-zero now.

Scope-shift detection: zero signals (panel clean, grill decisions were unit-contract refinements not plan-structure changes, no round-2 whiteboard, U2 is not the phase's last unit). No revise offered.
#72 (Phase 2 U1) pr-opened + pr-merged reconciled into this commit per the per-unit cadence recipe.
